title: Counterposition Durability

content.statement: Sustainable advantage comes from doing something competitors can't copy without harming themselves.
content.question: What is one thing you could build into your business that a larger competitor would lose money by copying?
content.equation: CD = ((Incumbent Pain × Margin Advantage × Adoption) ÷ Replication Ease) × 10
sop: For each, name the thing you do that they structurally can't copy without hurting their own business
Rate Incumbent Pain, Margin Advantage, and Adoption for each, 1-10 (see calculation, page 6)
Double down on the highest-scoring counterposition
content.explainer: Counterposition is the rarest moat. Not a secret, a poison pill. If your model cannibalizes theirs, they delay or ignore you until it's too late.
questions: What is one thing you could build into your business that a larger competitor would lose money by copying?
If a well-funded copycat cloned your product tomorrow, what would it actually cost them to use it?
Where does your advantage come from something a competitor is structurally stuck with, not just something you're currently better at?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Incumbent Pain 8, Margin Advantage 6, Adoption 7, divided by Replication Ease 3, times 10 = 1,120. Higher scores mean a harder-to-copy, more durable edge. Lower scores mean a competitor could copy you at little cost to themselves.

definitions.def: A position your competitor structurally cannot copy without harming themselves. Different from being merely better, copying you must actively cost them more than ignoring you.
A durable competitive advantage that protects your business from competition over time.
The existing, established player in a market, the one with the most to lose if a new entrant succeeds.
When a product takes sales away from another product instead of adding new ones. A rival copying you would cannibalize their own existing revenue.

content.closing_statement: The strongest edge costs a rival more to copy than to ignore.

caption:
Principle Nº 1: Counterposition Durability

Sustainable advantage comes from doing something competitors can't copy without harming themselves. Counterposition is the rarest moat.

Ask yourself: What is one thing you could build into your business that a larger competitor would lose money by copying?
